chapter: JEF P101 – A Triumph of Surgery
title: A Triumph of Surgery – Medium VSAQs

questions.question: Why was Tricki listless at the beginning of the story?
questions.answer: Because he was overfed and had no exercise.

questions.question: What advice did the vet give Mrs Pumphrey initially?
questions.answer: To reduce food and increase exercise.

questions.question: Why did the vet hospitalise Tricki?
questions.answer: To strictly control his routine and diet.

questions.question: What was the first sign of Tricki’s recovery?
questions.answer: He began to take interest in his surroundings.

questions.question: Why did the vet give Tricki no food for two days?
questions.answer: To let his system recover naturally.

questions.question: What role did other dogs play in Tricki’s recovery?
questions.answer: They encouraged exercise and activity.

questions.question: Why did Mrs Pumphrey frequently call the vet?
questions.answer: She was anxious about Tricki’s condition.

questions.question: What did the eggs and wine symbolise?
questions.answer: Mrs Pumphrey’s mistaken idea of care.

questions.question: How did Tricki behave at mealtime later?
questions.answer: He competed eagerly with other dogs.

questions.question: Why is the vet tempted to keep Tricki longer?
questions.answer: Because of the luxury food sent by Mrs Pumphrey.

questions.question: What change in Tricki shows complete recovery?
questions.answer: He became active and playful.

questions.question: Why does Mrs Pumphrey think it is a medical miracle?
questions.answer: She believes surgery cured Tricki.

questions.question: What does the vet’s silence show?
questions.answer: His tact and kindness.

questions.question: How is humour used in the story?
questions.answer: To criticise excess affection gently.

questions.question: What is the central conflict of the story?
questions.answer: Emotion versus reason.

questions.question: Why is the title suitable?
questions.answer: It highlights irony in Tricki’s recovery.

questions.question: What quality of the vet ensures success?
questions.answer: Practical wisdom.

questions.question: Why is Mrs Pumphrey portrayed sympathetically?
questions.answer: Her actions come from love and loneliness.

questions.question: What lesson does Tricki’s illness teach?
questions.answer: Excess can be harmful.

questions.question: What does the surgery environment represent?
questions.answer: Discipline and routine.

questions.question: Why does Tricki refuse food initially?
questions.answer: His body was overloaded.

questions.question: What literary tone dominates the story?
questions.answer: Gentle humour.

questions.question: How does the vet balance truth and kindness?
questions.answer: By not correcting Mrs Pumphrey harshly.

questions.question: Why is Tricki’s recovery natural?
questions.answer: It is due to lifestyle change.

questions.question: What value does the story promote?
questions.answer: Moderation.
